name: Frank Cairone
summary: Frank Cairone is a young left-handed pitcher drafted by the Milwaukee Brewers in the 2025 supplemental second round. He features a fastball up to 95 mph that plays up due to his delivery and a plus-potential slider with high spin. Considered a breakout candidate for 2026, he will need to refine his command and build endurance to maintain velocity. He recently suffered injuries in a car accident and his recovery may impact the start of his professional debut.
strengths: Plus potential slider and fastball velocity

recentNews.title: Frank Cairone News | RotoWire
recentNews.summary: Frank Cairone was involved in a serious car accident on January 2nd, sustaining injuries that are expected to delay his professional debut with an estimated return date of June 1, 2026.

recentNews.title: Brewers GM Reveals Promising Update On Prospect After Scary Incident
recentNews.summary: Milwaukee Brewers president of baseball operations Matt Arnold provided an encouraging update on pitching prospect Frank Cairone, stating that he is 'progressing positively' in his recovery after a serious car accident in New Jersey.

recentNews.title: Frank Cairone, Brewers' prospect and N.J. native, injured in car crash
recentNews.summary: Frank Cairone, a Brewers prospect and South Jersey native, was injured in a serious car crash and is currently being treated at a hospital.

recentNews.title: Brewers 2025 second-round pick cited as mostly likely 2026 breakout prospect
recentNews.summary: Frank Cairone, the Brewers' 2025 second-round pick, was identified as the team's top breakout prospect candidate for the 2026 season, with analysts detailing his intriguing pitching repertoire and potential for an aggressive assignment to Single-A.

recentNews.title: Frank Cairone Stats & Scouting Report - Baseball America
recentNews.summary: A scouting report on Frank Cairone highlights his promising projection indicators, including a velocity spike, low-80s slider with high spin, and potential for further velocity increase, positioning him as one of the best breakout candidates in the Brewers' lower minor league system for 2026.
